body:
Cold boot test #2 + both pending task sets: CONFIRMED.

(1) RelayListener auto-launched with no prior session open -- listener.log shows ticks continuing through the close window, cold-start fired on schedule, no manual launch.
(2) bootup-recall ran: no .checkpoint/.pinned files existed yet (first time these would be populated), so nothing to resume -- that part of the harness is wired but has nothing written to it yet. Worth a save-checkpoint step if you want true continuity next test.
(3) All 3 mailbox messages found and processed with zero manual input.

Task set (msgs 2+3, reconciled -- msg 3 arrived 33s after msg 2 and explicitly superseded its window-focus ask, so I followed msg 3's spec):
1. POLLING 30s: Windows Task Scheduler hard-rejects sub-1-minute RepetitionInterval at the XML schema level (confirmed live: 'task XML contains a value which is incorrectly formatted or out of range' on PT30S). True 30s on one task isn't possible on Windows. Fix: registered two tasks (RelayListener + RelayListenerOffset), same action, both repeating every 1 min, start times offset by 30s -- combined effective cadence is 30s. Both live-triggered and confirmed LastTaskResult=0.
2. WINDOW STYLE: relay-listener.ps1's cold-start Start-Process now uses -WindowStyle Normal instead of -WindowStyle Minimized -- the Claude Code terminal opens fully visible and focused. The wscript/VBS layer that hides the 30s listener-tick poll is untouched and still fully hidden (window style 0), per msg 3's distinction between the two layers.

Also handled your follow-up: C:\Users\User had hasTrustDialogAccepted=false in .claude.json -- that's exactly why cold-boot launches were stopping on a manual 1/2 trust prompt. Set it to true. Cold-boot should now run with zero manual input going forward.

Source scripts updated at the canonical path, deployed to $HOME, tasks unregistered/re-registered, all live-verified. Memory updated (reference_relay_listener.md, new feedback_device_trust_permanent.md).
